title: practical english usage - Part 290
content:
3  imperatives         and   short    answers that also / as well I too refers to.

When we  speak, we  show the exact meaning by stressing the word or expression c   (We work on other days, and) we  work on Saturdays as well.

(We do other things on Saturdays, and) we  work on Saturdays as well.

(Other people work on Saturdays, and) we  work on Saturdays as well.

Consider the sentence We work on Saturdays as well. This can mean three These words can refer to different parts of a clause, depending on the meaning.

It's a nice house, but it's very small. Also, it needs a lot of repairs.

beginning of a clause to give more importance to a new piece of information.

As well and too do not go at the beginning of a clause. Also can go at the She doesn't just sing she plays the piano too.

She not only sings; she plays the piano as well.

She not only sings; she also plays the piano.

American English.

as well and too usually go at the end of a clause. As well is less common in position in clauses. Also usually goes with the verb, in mid-position (►   200); Also, as well and too have similar meanings, but they do not go in the same 1  position 369 also,     as   well     and   too   ____________ vocabulary «371      although, though, but and however, contrast For however in sentences like However much  he eats, he never gets fat, ►    25 2.

For sentences like Cold though it was, I went out, ►    255.

For as though, ►    378.   For even though (     even-aMwugh), ►    452.4.

The strongest argument, though, is economic and not political.

'Nice day.' 'Yes. Bit cold, though.' to mean ‘ however’ .

We   can use though as an adverb (often at the end of a sentence), 4 though        used    as  an   adverb The police did not believe him,  however.

The police, however, did not believe him.

However, the police did not believe him.

by one or two commas, depending on its position.

However can go in various positions. It is normally separated from its sentence above examples.

the one before. This is why  it comes after a full stop or a semi-colon in the second. However is an adverb: it does not connect its sentence grammatically to But is a conjunction: it joins two clauses, and comes at the beginning of the 3 but    and   however:         the   difference (A) It is a bit late; however, (B) I'd quite like to go out.

(A) It is a bit late, but (B) I'd quite like to go out.

(A) I don't like him. However, (B) I agree that he's a good manager.

(A) I don't like him,  but (B) I agree that he's a good manager.

‘ unexpected’  clause (*B’ ).

We   can give the same meaning by putting but or however with the contrasting, 2  but    and   however (B) I'd quite like to go out, (although (A) it is a bit late.

(Al)though (A) I don't like him,  (B) I agree that he's a good manager.

surprising about ‘ B‘ .

contrast. When  we  say ‘ (Al)though A, B’ , there is something unexpected or writing. They  introduce an idea (‘ A ’ ) with which  the main clause (‘ B’ ) is in Though is less formal than although, and is more common in speech than Both these words can be used as conjunctions, with the same meaning.

1 although          and   though:       conjunctions contrast     _______ ___ ___________ 371 although,          though,        but    and   however.

as alternative(ly).

In American English, alternatc(ly) can be used with the same meaning You could go by air, or alternatively you could drive there.

Jessica's not free on the 27 th. We'll have to find an alternative date.

Alternatively) is similar to ‘ different’ , ‘ instead’ , ‘ on the other hand’ .

Word  Problems from A  to Z      Section 31 vocabulary •  372   altogether and all together not He appears (to be) very angry today, (     He appears very angrily-today!) appear or appear to be.

With this meaning, appear is followed by adjectives, not adverbs. We   can use an informal style.

used in similar ways to seem (►  576 for details), but is less frequent, especially in Appear can be a linking verb (►    11), used to say how  things look or seem. It is 1 linking     verb:   'seem' 374 appear Was the film any good?  This watch is no use. It keeps stopping.
